records.ety: [Cf. F. tyranniser, Gr. tyranni`zein to take the part of tyrants.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Tyrannized (tr"n*nīzd); p. pr. & vb. n. Tyrannizing (tr"n*nī`zng).]

defs: To act the tyrant; to exercise arbitrary power; to rule with unjust and oppressive severity; to exercise power over others not permitted by law or required by justice, or with a severity not necessary to the ends of justice and government; as, a prince will often tyrannize over his subjects; masters sometimes tyrannize over their servants or apprentices.
To subject to arbitrary, oppressive, or tyrannical treatment; to oppress.
